en.title: Group Defends Pacific Limited Over Limbe Power Cut
en.body: A group called MaBLEM says Pacific Limited did not cause a blackout in Limbe during the rainy season. The group visited the site and checked what happened. Heavy rain damaged an ESCOM electricity wall. The wall fell and broke electricity poles. This caused the power to go down for one day. MaBLEM says the company did the work on its own land and followed all the rules.
en.body_more:
MaBLEM checked the area in Blantyre where the blackout happened. The group says heavy rains were the main cause of the problem. ESCOM poles and walls sit on high ground near Pacific Limited's new shopping building. The rain was very strong. Water and mud pushed the wall down. The poles fell and broke the power lines. This is what caused the blackout. MaBLEM also found that heavy rains damaged more than 28 ESCOM power installations across Malawi at the same time.

The group says Pacific Limited is being treated unfairly. Many people and businesses in Malawi blame the company for problems that happen. MaBLEM says this is not fair to local companies. Pacific Limited is owned by Malawians and creates many jobs. The company built Pacific Towers in Limbe. More than 200 black-owned shops now work there. The building gives cheap space to local business owners.

MaBLEM also says Pacific Limited fixed more than 10,000 boreholes across Malawi. These boreholes give clean water to people in villages and poor areas. The group says the company also built an underground tunnel under its own building. This tunnel helps ESCOM move power lines in the future. This shows that Pacific Limited works well with ESCOM. MaBLEM asked all media, leaders, and the public to report facts about local companies. They must be fair and not use angry words or guesses.

ny.title: MaBLEM Ikunyoza Pacific Limited Pa Kupsa kwa Lamulachi
ny.body: Gulu yotchedwa MaBLEM liyankula kuti Pacific Limited sinadala kupsa kwa lamulachi mu Limbe pothawi ndula. Gulu limayika kuti ndula yayikulu ndiye yaidala vuto, osati kampani. Minda yandula inapweteka pa m'mwalire wa ESCOM. Pampiyipiti yapaduka ndiyo idagwetsela. Ichi chinayambitsa kupsa kwa lamulachi pa tsiku limodzi. MaBLEM imanena kuti kampani inapanga ntchito yake molungamilira ndi molondoloza mfundo.
ny.body_more:
MaBLEM idapita pa Blantyre pomene kupsa kunatchedwere. Gulu linena kuti ndula yayikulu ndi chinthu chachikulu chamavuto onsewa. Paapoloji a ESCOM ali pamwamba pa dziko linene kumbali mwa nyumba yatsopano ya Pacific Limited. Ndula inafulumira kwambiri. Madzi anapweteka m'mwalire wankhondo. M'mwalire unapaduka kumbali konse. Malo onsewa apeza kupsa. Ichi ndi chimene chidagwetsela kupsa. MaBLEM komanso inapeza kuti ndula inapweteka kumalawi konse. Malo koposa 28 a ESCOM anafunjikidwa nthawi yomweyo.

Gulu lachisankho linena kuti Pacific Limited ikulangidwa molusa. Anthu ndi mabizinesi ambiri mu Malawi akudzionera kampani pa zinthu zomwe zimachitika. MaBLEM imanena iti izi siziwona bwino kampaniye yotchedwa Malawi. Pacific Limited imagulitsidwa ndi anthu a Malawi ndipo ipangitsa ntchito zambiri. Kampani inamanga Pacific Towers mu Limbe. Bizinesi 200 kuposa izipanga ndi anthu akuda komweko. Nyumbayi ipetsera malo ang'onoang'ono pa anthu omaliza pano.

MaBLEM komanso imanena kuti Pacific Limited inarepala zotheka zamanzi 10,000 kuposa mu Malawi. Zitheka izi zipatsira madzi olondoloza anthu mu mitengo ndi m'malo osakonda. Gulu linena kuti kampani inakumbiranso underground tunnel pansi pa nyumba yake yeniyeni. Tunnel iyi iyampatsa ESCOM njira yosunsa zopakira lamulachi. Izi zikuwonetsa kuti Pacific Limited imalankhula bwino ndi ESCOM. MaBLEM idaanena kwa media ndi nzika ndi anthu onse kuti azindikira maumbo ovuta pamakampani. Ayenera kukhala olungamilira ndi asamagwire mano ovuta kapena kulira.

en.title: Bullets beat Wanderers 2-1 in fierce derby match
en.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets won 2-1 against Mighty Wanderers on Sunday at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. This was a big win for Bullets. They had lost four games in a row to Wanderers before this match. Blessings Mpokera scored first from a penalty in the fifth minute. Maxwell Gasten Phodo scored the second goal in the 58th minute. Promise Kamwendo scored one goal for Wanderers in the 61st minute.
en.body_more:
The match was very tense and had many angry moments. In the first half, players Ephraim Kondowe and Dan Kudonto were both given yellow cards after they fought each other. In the second half, things got worse. Wanderers played hard and hit the post three times. But Bullets scored their second goal when Phodo headed in from a corner kick. Three minutes later, Wanderers got a penalty and Kamwendo scored to make it 2-1.

The game became very rough near the end. A Bullets official named Noel Kaole was sent off in the 69th minute. Then Phodo and Gaddie Chirwa from Wanderers were both sent off after they fought each other on the field. Wanderers threw everything forward to try to score again, but Bullets' goalkeeper Innocent Nyasulu made good saves to keep the score at 2-1.

The match made a lot of money for the teams. The stadium collected K90.8 million in total. After all costs were paid, the teams shared K36.6 million equally. Each team got K18.3 million. Analyst Ibrahim Phiri said the money was lower than expected because some people may have got into the stadium without paying. Bullets now has seven points from three games and is top of the table. Wanderers has three points from two games.

ny.title: Bullets amapambana Wanderers 2-1 m'masewero akali okwera
ny.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets anapambana Mighty Wanderers kwa masewero a 2-1 pa Mulungu pa Bingu National Stadium mu Lilongwe. Ichi chinali chipambano chachikulu kwa Bullets. Iwo anali kuwukwa ndi Wanderers m'masewero anayi a mzere. Blessings Mpokera anagola chigoli choyamba kuchokera pa penote mu mphindi ya zisanu. Maxwell Gasten Phodo anagola chigoli chachiwiri mu mphindi ya 58. Promise Kamwendo anagola chigoli limodzi cha Wanderers mu mphindi ya 61.
ny.body_more:
Masewero anali okwera kwambiri ndipo anali ndi chiyembekezo chambiri. M'chigawo choyamba, ochita masewero Ephraim Kondowe ndi Dan Kudonto adagula mbalala pawiri chifukwa cha kumenyana. M'chigawo chachiwiri, zinthu zidamala. Wanderers anachita mochepa kwambiri ndipo adavala zitsulo zitatu. Koma Bullets anagola chigoli chachiwiri pomwe Phodo anayambitsa mu mphindi ya 58 kuchokera pa kona. Mphindi zitatu zaka, Wanderers anadza ndi penote ndipo Kamwendo anagola kupereka 2-1.

Masewero adakhala okali kwambiri pambali pake. Munthu wamphunzitsi a Bullets amatchedwa Noel Kaole anatulutsa m'masewero mu mphindi ya 69. Atha iye, Phodo wa Bullets ndi Gaddie Chirwa wa Wanderers adatulutsa pambuyo pa kumenyana pamunda. Wanderers anagwiritsa mpira iwonso yonse kuti azamala kugola chigoli, koma Innocent Nyasulu womwonya wa Bullets anamangidwa bwino kuti zitsulo siziphike.

Masewero amapangitsa ndalama zambiri. Mundandanda unamutsa ndalama K90.8 miliyoni. Pambuyo pa kuwononga kosewera, timu zidagawana ndalama K36.6 miliyoni mobwera bwera. Timu iliyonse inapeza ndalama K18.3 miliyoni. Analyst wamatchedwa Ibrahim Phiri anatani kuti ndalama zinali zochepa chifukwa anthu angapo anazingira mu mundandanda popanda kulipiritsa. Bullets tsopano ili ndi ma pointi 7 kuchokera mu masewero atatu ndipo ili pamwamba pa ndandanda. Wanderers ili ndi ma pointi atatu kuchokera mu masewero awiri.

en.title: Malawi mining boom: who will benefit most?
en.body: Malawi is set to earn between $30 billion and $43 billion from mining over the next 14 years. The country has rare earth deposits at Kangankunde and rutile at Kasiya. These minerals are needed for electric cars and clean energy. But many people ask: who will really get the money and who will pay the cost?
en.body_more:
The Balaka mining project at Kangankunde is expected to make $114 million every year. But Balaka District Council gets nothing from this money. The council still has to run the district and pay for schools and roads. This is because the law does not give mining money to local councils.

Bertha Phiri heads the Malawi Economic Justice Network. She said the mining laws need to change. She wants district councils to get part of the money from mining. She also said people must know what mining companies agree to do. Mining expert Grain Malunga said Malawi forgot about local content. This means using local workers and local goods in mining work. He said district councils have not got ready for this.

Balaka District Commissioner Bibu Yusufu Mdala said the council gets only K586.6 million each year but needs to spend K55.8 billion. The council depends on money from the central government. Most mining money now goes to central government or to companies. Only when a company signs a Community Development Agreement does the local area get money—just 0.45 percent of yearly revenue.

Governance expert Henry Chingayipe said the country should use a Sovereign Wealth Fund for mining money. He said much of this money should go to towns and villages near mines, to research, and to build better roads and buildings. The Malawi Mining Regulatory Authority has not yet answered questions about these proposals.

ny.title: Mabingu a Malawi: ndani ayamba ndalama?
ny.body: Malawi idzapeza ndalama kuchokera mabingu pakati pa dola 30 biliyoni ndi 43 biliyoni mofuta miyezi 14 yotsatira. Dziko lili ndi zinthu zochokera pa dziko monga rare earth pa Kangankunde komanso rutile pa Kasiya. Zinthuzi zimafunika kuti apange magalimoto atsopano ndi mphamvu yabwino. Koma anthu ambiri amapitirizira: ndani atha kugwira ndalama ndipo ndani atha kulankhula?
ny.body_more:
Nchito ya mabingu pa Balaka kumpulumutzira kwa Kangankunde idzapangitsa kuti lipeze dola 114 miliyoni pa chaka. Koma Balaka District Council salifera ndalama imeneyi. Dera likadali lili ndi ntchito zambiri monga kukhala ndi masikolo ndi milatho. Ndichifukwa chakuti lamulo sizingathe kuperekanso ndalama za mabingu kwa masikelo..

Bertha Phiri akutsogolera gulu lomwe anthu atha kukhala ndi ndalama mobwino. Iye anati mzimu wamabingu ufunika kusinthidwa. Iye ndi akufuna kuti masikelo azidzilandira gawo la ndalama zomwe abingu apanga. Iyenso anati anthu ayenera kuzindikira zomwe kampuni iza lolembedwa. Mloyidawi wa mabingu Grain Malunga anati Malawi sanaganizire za magalimoto ndi zipiliya zanu mmene mabingu akutsogoleredwa. Iye anati masikelo sanakonzekera kwa izi.

Mtsogoleri wa Balaka District Bibu Yusufu Mdala anati likelo limapeza K586.6 miliyoni pa chaka koma limafunika K55.8 biliyoni. Dera lomaliza lile ndi ndalama zomwe boma lalikumana ndi mabingu. Ndalama yambiri iyamapita kwa boma lapakati kapena kwa makampuni. Ndizomwe kampsuni yili kuti ione msonkhano wathanzi wakuti dera lipeze K0.45 pa zana za ndalama pa chaka.

Mvoti ya kusankhana mungalande Henry Chingayipe anati dziko lili linayenera kugwiritsa ntchito ndalamazo bwino ndi kungopatsa magawo amakomo ndi kumene mabingu akuchokera, kuphunzitsa, ndi kumandimutz milatho ndi nyumba. Bungwe la Malawi la mabingu silinayankhule kufunsidwe za njirazi.

en.title: Malawi's mining boom: who gets the money?
en.body: Malawi is about to dig for rare minerals. The country has gold, rare earth metals and other minerals. Mining could bring $30 to $43 billion in fourteen years. But people ask: Who will benefit? Poor people who live near the mines may not get much. Laws say people must be told before mining starts. But in reality, many communities do not understand what is happening.
en.body_more:
The Kangankunde mine in Balaka District shows the problem. This mine will make $114 million each year. But Balaka District Council will get zero money. The council has very little money to run schools and fix roads. It must ask the central government for help.

The problem is that current law does not share mining money with local councils. Only if a mining company signs a special agreement with the community does the local area get 0.45 percent of the money. Bertha Phiri works for a justice group. She said the laws must change. She said district councils must get part of the mining money. She also said communities must see all mining contracts.

Grain Malunga is a mining expert. He said Malawi has not focused enough on using local workers and local materials in mines. "Local people must have jobs in mining," he said. But district councils are not ready to train workers or run mines well. They do not have enough money or skills.

Balaka District has a budget of K55.8 billion each year. But it can only collect K586.6 million itself. The rest comes from the government and donors. This means the district cannot do much without help from above. Mining money could change this. The government says it will think about these ideas. But the Mining Authority has not yet answered questions about sharing money fairly with local areas.

ny.title: Mabwino azipele mu Malawi: ndani anagwira ndalama?
ny.body: Malawi likupita kuswala migodi. Nsi ili ili ndi zinthu zingapo zomwe zimakopa kwambiri. Kuswala kumatha kupangitsa kuti nsi iyi ipeze ndalama zoposa zilliyoni za madola 30 mpaka 43 mchaka chake chisanu ndi chisanu. Koma anthu akufunsa: Ndani amapeza ndalama? Anthu oyika kumipata ya migodi sangapeze pindipindiyana. Lamulo likunena kuti anthu ayenera kuuzidwa musanasiyeni kuswala. Koma mu gawo lenileni, gulu lochuluka la anthu silimvera zake.
ny.body_more:
Migodi ya Kangankunde mu Balaka imawonetsa vuto. Migodi iyi iyenera kupezetsa ndalama za zilliyoni 114 za madola chaka chilichonse. Koma bungwe la District la Balaka sipeze ndalama. Bungwe ili lili ndi ndalama yochepa yokoneza sukulu ndi njira.

Vuto ndi lokuti lamulo logachokera pomene ligwire ndalama siligawani ndalama kwa ma district councils omwe akumambitsa migodi. Chinthu chokhacho ndi ngati kampuni ya migodi isainidwe pa pepala lokuti imapereka magawo a magawo. Ndipo kupertekera, kompani imapereka 0.45 peresenti yazaka zonse. Bertha Phiri akusambitsira anthu. Iye anati lamulo likuyenera kusinthidwa. Anati ma district councils ayenera kugawana ndalama zomwe zimapezeka mu migodi. Anati anzanthu ayenera kuwona zinthu zonse zimene zimaloledwa mu migodi.

Grain Malunga ndi wodziwika mu migodi. Iye anati Malawi sadzambutsa kwambiri kukubweza anthu a uko kuno akupita kulankhula kugwira ntchito mu migodi. "Anthu a uko kuno ayenera kukhala ndi ndalama zimene amapeza kugwira ntchito mu migodi," anati iye. Koma ma district councils salungamilira mokwanira. Alibe ndalama yokwanira kuti apereke maphunziro kale.

Balaka District ilida ndalama ya zilliyoni 55.8 za kwacha mu zaka. Koma ikungogonana kupezeketsa zilliyoni 586.6 chokha. Zina zimachokera mu bungwe la central ndi anthu omwe amapereka. Izi ndi zisango: kuswala ndalama kumatha kuthandiza. Bungwe la government likunena kuti liyenera kuganizira mano amenewa. Koma wosambitsira migodi sapemphe funso lililonse pamapeto pano.

en.title: Former Finance Minister Banda Rejects Arrest Claims
en.body: Simplex Chithyola Banda, a former Finance Minister, says he is not running from the law. He says reports that he is evading arrest are false and politically motivated. The Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) has issued a warrant for his arrest. Banda's team says he left Lilongwe on Friday evening before he knew about the warrant. They say he will work with the ACB when they contact him properly.
en.body_more:
The ACB says Banda approved about K29.5 billion without proper approval. The money went to the Mega Farms Project and the Greenbelt Authority. An ACB spokesperson, Jacqueline Ngongonda, said the funds left government procedures the wrong way. A court in Lilongwe issued the warrant on May 8, 2026.

Banda's helpers say he did not get any letter from the ACB before he travelled. They say it is unusual for police to try to arrest someone at night without talking to them first. They say normal practice is to contact the person and let them come in themselves. Banda's team says he is calm and ready to work with investigators.

Banda has faced other legal troubles recently. On April 13, police arrested him over the East Bridge fertilizer deal. He was accused of abuse of office and money laundering in that case too. Banda's helpers say he presented himself to police before, and he will do the same now. They say the reports that Banda is running are not true and damage his name without fair process.

ny.title: Chithyola Banda Akana Kuti Akumenyedwa, Akuonetsa Ufulu
ny.body: Simplex Chithyola Banda, yemwe anali Minister wa Finance, akana kuti akumenyedwa ndi lamulo. Akuti manyuzipepala akuti akumenyedwa ndi Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) ndi mabra osalola. ACB idasindika warrant ya kumukuta. Anzake a Banda akuti iye anasiya Lilongwe Jumamosi mangʼanja pomwe sanayike za warrant ino. Akuti iye akuonetsa ufulu kusambirana ndi ACB pamene idzamutembulutsa molondola.
ny.body_more:
ACB ikuti Banda anaamitiritsa pera K29.5 billion osalamula bwino. Ndalama zija zidapita ku Mega Farms Project ndi Greenbelt Authority. Wolankhula wa ACB, Jacqueline Ngongonda, akuti ndalama zija zisachitike mwamunthu wolamula. Court ya Lilongwe idasindika warrant pa May 8, 2026.

Anzake a Banda akuti iye sanapezepo ulimi wanji kuchokera ACB isanasiye Lilongwe. Akuti sizachitika kuti anthu a police akayese kumukuta usiku osayangoyankhula naye pang'ono. Akuti mwamunthu amafunika kuyankha ndi kumva kuti umene ankayende. Anzake a Banda akuti iye ali ndi mtima womilala ndipo akuonetsa ufulu kusambirana ndi adule ake.

Banda anagwirita ntchito ya milandu ingʼina kwinakwino. Pa April 13, police anamamutsani pamalonda a East Bridge fertilizer. Anakambidwa ndi mabra amene agwira ntchito yake osalama ndi kuneneramo kwa ndalama. Anzake a Banda akuti iye anathandizira police kale, ndipo adzachita chiyani ichi. Akuti manyuzipepala akuti akumenyedwa ndi osadikira ndipo akumenyera dzina lake osalamula.

en.title: Ex-Finance Minister Says He Is Not Running From Police
en.body: Simplex Chithyola Banda, a former Finance Minister, says he is not hiding from the law. The Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) has said it has a warrant to arrest him. But Chithyola says the reports are false and wrong. He says he left the city on Friday evening before he knew about the warrant. He says he will help the ACB.
en.body_more:
The ACB says Chithyola took about 29.5 billion kwacha of government money. This money was for the Mega Farms Project, the ACB says. The warrant was signed by the Lilongwe court on May 8, 2026. ACB spokesperson Jacqueline Ngongonda said Chithyola gave money without good reason. The money went to the Greenbelt Authority, she said.

Chithyola's helpers say he did nothing wrong. They say he left Lilongwe on Friday night. The warrant came later that same night, they say. They also say the ACB did not call him first. They say this is not how the law works. They say suspects get a call before police come at night. "The ACB did not call him on Friday," his aide said. "We think the ACB will call him today."

Chithyola's team says he is not a man who runs away. They say when police came for him before, in April, he went to them. "He will come when you ask him," his aide said. "People who say he is hiding are wrong."

This is the second time in weeks that Chithyola faces arrest. On April 13, police from the Fiscal and Fraud section took him in over other charges. Those charges were about money and abuse of office. They link to a fertilizer deal called East Bridge. Chithyola says he did not break the law in that case either.

ny.title: Chithyola Akunka Kuti Salayo Ponya Mfundo Za Mzinda
ny.body: Simplex Chithyola Banda, ministan wa mapeto kale, anena kuti sana salayo. Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) inati ili ndi mwayi wothetsa iye. Koma Chithyola anena kuti mawu akusowa ndi osakwiya. Anena kuti adamasuza lililongwe pa Mlauni ndipo panaali uziwitso za mwayi uja. Anena kuti adzathandiza ACB.
ny.body_more:
ACB inena kuti Chithyola anatengela pesa zosakubvira K29.5 biliyoni za boma. Pesa iyi inali yopangidwa pa Mega Farms Project, ACB inawu. Mwayi unasainidwa ndi Lilongwe court pa Meyi 8, 2026. ACB yosesa Jacqueline Ngongonda anena kuti Chithyola adappha pesa popanda chipangizo chokoyika. Pesa idapita kwa Greenbelt Authority, iyenso.

Aanthu a Chithyola anena kuti sana salayo. Anena kuti adamasuza Lilongwe pa Mlauni kale. Mwayi unabvela nthawi ija inene, iyenso anena. Anena kuti ACB sanamupikilira kale. Anena kuti ino siyi njira ya lamulo. Anthu omwe woyiwala mwayi aziteledwa kale. "ACB sanamupikilira pa Mlauni," mudzukulu wa iye. "Tikuganizirah kuti ACB adzamupikilira Lolemba."

Aanthu a Chithyola anena kuti sana si muntu wokumaputa. Anena kuti nthawi maapolisi aja kale, mu Epulo, adapita kwa iwo. "Adzapita ngati mumukole," mudzukulu wa iye. "Anthu akuti aputa ndi osakwiya."

Uyu ndi nthawi yachiwiri mumpikisani kale kuti Chithyola ngomalira nthawiyi. Pa Epulo 13, apolisi amene amagwira ndalama anachita kuti atewe. Mfunozo zinali za ndalama ndi kumukonda bwino nzeru. Zikugwirizana ndi msonkhano wa ufa uja unaitanidwa East Bridge. Chithyola anena kuti sana salayo mumpiko iyi.

en.title: Artists ask for more help to grow the art industry
en.body: Visual artists in Malawi want more support and money for their work. They made this call at Art in the Park 2026, an event held at Four Seasons Gardens in Lilongwe from Friday to Sunday. Artists said there are not enough spaces to show their work. They also asked people to buy local art and help the creative industry grow.
en.body_more:
Artist Charles Levison said more platforms are needed so people can see and buy art. "If we have more spaces to show our work, more people will learn about art and want to buy it," he said. Another artist, Panji Tembo, said that visitors from other countries like Malawian art very much, but local people do not buy enough. He asked for more art events in cities like Blantyre to help grow the industry.

Standard Bank gave K20 million to help pay for Art in the Park. The bank's chief, Phillip Madinga, said growth is not only about money—it is also about art and culture. "We must invest in artists and help Malawi's talent grow here and in other countries," he said. Madinga told people to buy Malawian art and put it on their walls at home and in offices.

Artists also said people must buy art outside of events. Timothy Kamayi said: "Support must not stop after exhibitions. People should go to art galleries and buy works there." Mbanase Nyirenda agreed, saying Malawians should change how they think about art.

Nick Hall, who leads the Wildlife and Environmental Society of Malawi, said the art standards are getting better each year. At this year's event, 47 artists showed their work. Money from the sales will help pay for wildlife clubs in schools. The government also said it will set up a National Arts and Heritage Council to help the creative sector grow.

ny.title: Oimba akunena kuti akufunika thandizo lochezekacho
ny.body: Anthu omwe amapanga zojambula mu Malawi akufuna thandizo ndi ndalama zambiri. Anayankhula izi pa msonkhano wa Art in the Park 2026 wobadwa pa Four Seasons Gardens m'Lilongwe kuyambira Lachisanu mpaka Mulungu. Oimba anati palibe malo ambiri okweza zojambula zawo. Anamwaipitsitsa anthu kuti agule zojambula za Malawi kuti industi ya seni ikule.
ny.body_more:
Oimba Charles Levison anati akufunika malo ambiri kuti awonetse zojambula zawo. "Ngati tili ndi malo ambiri, anthu ambiri adzadziwa za zojambula ndipo adzachita kugula," anati. Oimba wina, Panji Tembo, anati anthu a kumtundu wina amakonda zojambula za Malawi kwambiri, koma anthu a mu Malawi sakugula kwambiri. Anati akufunika zocheza monga Art in the Park pa mizinda monga Blantyre kuti industi ikhale ndi inu.

Standard Bank inapereka ndalama za K20 miliyoni kuti azingilira msonkhano wa Art in the Park. Mlengi wa banki, Phillip Madinga, anati lipililo ndi la ndalama zokha—ndilopang'ono ndi zojambula ndi maphunziro. "Tiyenera kusankha anthu omwe amapanga zojambula ndi kuwalola kuti zimagwiridwe ntchito m'Malawi ndi kumtundu wina," anati. Madinga anamwaipitsitsa anthu kuti agule zojambula za Malawi ndikuziye pa khoma pa nyumba ndi ofisi.

Oimba anakwirankhulani kuti anthu akufunika kugula zojambula sichifukwa cha zocheza zokha. Timothy Kamayi anati: "Thandizo liyenera kuchirikira ndi kutsukila zocheza. Anthu ayenera kupita kwa malo omwe amgulitsa zojambula ndikugula." Mbanase Nyirenda anagwirizanatu, akuti Anthu a Malawi ayenera kukonza bwanji amawona zojambula.

Nick Hall, amene amalangiza Wildlife and Environmental Society of Malawi, anati zojambula zimakula bwino ndi bwino chaka ndi chaka. Msonkhanomu, oimba 47 anaonetsa zojambula zawo. Ndalama zomwe zidagulitsidwa zidzagwiritsidwa ntchito zomwe zikukweza kumodzimodzi ndi dziko.

en.title: Artists ask for more help to grow art in Malawi
en.body: Visual artists in Malawi have asked for more support and places to show their work. They made this call at Art in the Park 2026, a big art show that ended on Sunday in Lilongwe. The event ran from Friday to Sunday at Four Seasons Gardens. Artists said people in Malawi do not buy enough local art, but visitors from other countries do. They asked the government and companies to help the art industry grow.
en.body_more:
Artist Charles Levison said there are not enough spaces for artists to show what they make. He said people cannot appreciate art if they do not see it. Levison asked the government and private companies to give money like Standard Bank does. Another artist, Panji Tembo, said foreign visitors love Malawian art. He asked for the same kind of art shows to happen in other cities like Blantyre.

Charcoal artist Timothy Kamayi and painter Mbanase Nyirenda both said Malawians must buy art from galleries, not just at shows. They said this will help the industry grow all year long. Deputy Director of Arts MacDonald Maluwaya said he was happy with the art on show. He told the artists they are doing good work and some pieces sold for more than 2 million Kwacha.

Standard Bank gave 20 million Kwacha to help the event. The bank's leader, Phillip Madinga, said true growth means more than money. He said it means helping artists and keeping Malawi's culture alive. He asked people to put Malawian art on their walls. The Wildlife and Environmental Society of Malawi said 47 artists showed their work this year. The show had a theme about saving the environment. Money from the event will help make wildlife clubs in schools.

ny.title: Ojambula akufuna thandizo lachikhalidwe ndi ndalama
ny.body: Ojambula ku Malawi akufuna kuti anthu akuchita ntchito yachikhalidwe aphe ndalama zambiri. Ankafuna izi pa Art in the Park 2026, chomwe chinamalidwa Lingungulo lali mu Lilongwe. Chipangizochi chinachitika kuyambira Lachilaini mpaka Lingungulo pa Four Seasons Gardens. Ojambula ankati anthu a Malawi sakugula zojambula zachibadwa ine, koma anthu a ku zina amagutsimikiza. Ankafunira kuti boma ndi mabanja akulu athandize njira za chikhalidwe kuti lipile.
ny.body_more:
Ojambula Charles Levison ankati palibe malo ochuluka okukambira zojambula zawo. Ankati ngati anthu sawona zojambula, siziwona mwachangu. Levison anakumbirana boma ndi mabanja akulu kuti athe monga Standard Bank. Ojambula wina, Panji Tembo, ankati anthu akutali amapenda zojambula za Malawi kwambiri. Ankafuna kuti zipangizo zonga ziyesedwe mu mzinda wina monga Blantyre.

Ojambula wa galose Timothy Kamayi ndi pulani Mbanase Nyirenda ankati anthu a Malawi akufunire kugula zojambula ku magaleri, osati pa zipangizo zokha. Ankati izi zipatsanitse kuti njira iyi idzaongeza magulowe. Woyendetsa zojambula MacDonald Maluwaya ankati ankawona zojambula zabwino. Ankati ojambula akuchita ntchito yabwino komanso zojambula zambiri zidagulitsidwa ku K2 miliyoni.

Standard Bank inapereka ndalama za K20 miliyoni kuti ichitire chipangizo ichi. Mfumu wa bank, Phillip Madinga, ankati kulakula bwino sikuti ndalama yokha. Ankati kulakula kwambiri kumatchula kuti anthu akagwila ntchito yachikhalidwe. Ankati anthu akufunire kugwira zojambula pa madera a m'nyumba. Chipangizo chinachita ndi ojambula 47. Nkhani ya chipangizo inali yokhudzana ndi kusungira kalikali. Ndalama za chipangizo zizigwira ntchito yopanga magulu amafulu kuzikolo.

en.title: HIV cases rise in Nsanje District to 10.1 percent
en.body: HIV infections are rising in Nsanje District. The rate went up from 9 percent to 10.1 percent. McKnowledge Tembo is the HIV coordinator. He said this on Friday at an event. The church Word Alive Ministry held the event with help from Compassion International. It was at Mbeta village. Tembo said the district found 252 new infections in one year. About 25,000 people take HIV medicine now.
en.body_more:
Tembo said the rise is a worry. He said people must learn how to stop HIV. The district wants to reach the 95:95:95 target. This means 95 of 100 people know if they have HIV. Of those, 95 take medicine. Of those, 95 have the virus under control. Tembo said thank you to the church and Compassion International. They help give people the right facts about HIV.

Rex Mlawu is a police officer in Nsanje. He said the event was good. He said people got correct facts about how to stop HIV. The group village head also said thank you. He said many people got free health checks at the event.

Madalitso Benjamin works for Compassion International. He said the event is part of a bigger plan. The plan is called "Realising Children from Poverty in Jesus Christ". It helps young people. The plan gives money to help young people go to school and learn skills. Some young people learned to build with wood and brick. Some learned to make clothes. Some learned to fix lights and power.

The event tried to help people take their medicine. It also tried to stop new HIV cases. Malawi wants all people to reach the 95:95:95 target. New cases in some places are a problem for this work.

ny.title: Kachilombo ka HIV kachuluka mu Nsanje mpaka 10.1 pezenti
ny.body: Kachilombo ka HIV kachuluka mu Nsanje. Chiwerengero chochuluka ndi 10.1 pezenti kuchoka 9 pezenti. McKnowledge Tembo ndi woyendera nkhani za HIV. Anati izi pa Lachisanu pakhoni limene Word Alive Ministry International Church inakonzekera ndi Compassion International. Phoni linali ku Mbeta. Tembo anati chiwerengero chatsopano ndi 252 munthu m'chaka chimodzi. Pano anthu 25,000 amatha mankhwala a HIV.
ny.body_more:
Tembo anati kachuluka kuno n'chinthu choteseka. Anati anthu ayenera kudziwa momwe kachilombo kamenyedwa. Nsanje ikufexa kuchita zinthu 95:95:95. Izi zikutanthauza kuti anthu 95 pa 100 ankadziwa ngati ali ndi kachilombo. Mwa iwo, 95 amatha mankhwala. Mwa iwo, 95 mankhwala akudziwika bwino. Tembo anati asante Word Alive Ministry ndi Compassion International. Iwo amathandiza anthu kudziwa mabwinobwino a kachilombo.

Rex Mlawu ndi onjira ku Nsanje. Anati phoni linali labwino. Anati anthu anakuwa ndi mabwinobwino okhudzana ndi momwe kachilombo kamenyedwa. Mwini mudzi anakuwonanso. Anati anthu ochuluka anawona anzagubwino mwaulere panthoniyi.

Madalitso Benjamin akugwira ntchito ndi Compassion International. Anati phoniyi ndi mbali ya ntchito yaikulu. Ntchitoyi itchuka "Realising Children from Poverty in Jesus Christ". Iyo imathandiza ana aang'ono. Ntchitoyo imapatsa ndalama kuti ana aang'ono iyeni kusukulu ndi kuphunzira ukadaulo. Ana ena aphunzira kumanga ndi njerengete. Ena aphunzira kusita chovala. Ena aphunzira kumanga mipini ndi lampu.

Phoniyi inayamba kuti anthu amwamuke mankhwala awo. Inayambanso kuti anthu achepako achilombowe. Malawi akufexa kuchita zinthu 95:95:95 pa anthu onse. Kachilombo kakachuluka m'malo ena ndilo chinthu chopweteka.

en.title: Bullets Beat Wanderers 2-1 in Big Blantyre Derby
en.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets won 2-1 against Mighty Wanderers on Sunday at Bingu National Stadium. This was the 80th Blantyre derby — Malawi's biggest football game. Bullets had lost four times to Wanderers before. This win gives Bullets revenge and shows they are strong again.
en.body_more:
Bullets scored first in just four minutes. Ephraim Kondowe was knocked down inside the area. Blessings Mpokera took the penalty and scored. The ball went into the roof of the net. Wanderers' goalkeeper Chancy Mtete could not stop it.

Wanderers tried to fight back. Coach Bob Mpinganjira made changes to help his team. Clever Kalambo played well for Bullets on the wing. In the second half, Wanderers pushed hard for an equal goal. They hit the post three times. But in minute 58, Maxwell Gasten Phodo jumped high and scored from a corner. Wanderers got one goal back in minute 61. Promise Kamwendo scored from a penalty after a hand ball. This made the game very tight.

Things got ugly when Phodo and Gaddie Chirwa hit each other in minute 77. The referee showed them both red cards — they left the field. Wanderers pushed hard at the end but Bullets' goalkeeper Innocent Nyasulu made good saves. Nyasulu won the prize for best player of the match. After the game, Bullets' coach Gilbert Chirwa said this was perfect revenge. The win puts Bullets at the top of the league with seven points.

ny.title: Bullets Amagula Wanderers 2-1 pa Blantyre Derby
ny.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets anagula Mighty Wanderers 2-1 Lachisanu pa Bingu National Stadium. Ili ndi 80th Blantyre derby — masewera akulu kwambiri mu Malawi. Bullets anatha kuzindikira Wanderers zinthu zanu zitatu kale. Kulimbikitsa kumeneko kumabweretsa chikhulupiliro ku Bullets kachiwiri.
ny.body_more:
Bullets anafuna chilengedwe kumbuyo kwa magoli ganu angapo kale. Wanderers anawonka zinthu zinayi ndi Bullets mwaka wathawi. Mu mwezi uno, Wanderers anawonka thupi ndi Charity Shield. Koma pa Lachisanu, Bullets anabwera ndi mphindi yolimba.

Goli loyamba linaganiziridwa mu miniti 4. Ephraim Kondowe anagwa mkati mwa malo opepeza zothamanga. Blessings Mpokera anathamanga mpira wapepeza ndipo anafuna. Innocent Nyasulu ndi Bob Mpinganjira adangoona mpiramo. Wanderers adayesa kupereka kukunja. Koma Bullets anagona kusunga magoli. Mu miniti 58, Maxwell Gasten Phodo anakwera kwa mwayi ndipo analowetsa mpira wina. Wanderers adazindikira kuti zitali zosefasefala.

Mu miniti 61, Promise Kamwendo anafuna mpira wapepeza ndipo anafuna goli lina. Izo zimapanga masewera kukhala wopenza kwambiri. Gazeti lina likuti Phodo ndi Gaddie Chirwa anasonja mwala m'miniti 77. Mapulisimu anabwanji ife zoonse ziwiri. Wanderers adayesa kutsakika koma Nyasulu analola. Bullets anagula ndipo akulu ake Gilbert Chirwa adati izi ndi chilimbikitso chabwino. Goli lenilo limapanga Bullets kukhala pulotu pa legiwe ndi mapointi asanu.

en.title: Bullets face Wanderers in big derby match Sunday
en.body: Mighty Wanderers and FCB Nyasa Bullets will play each other on Sunday at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. This is the big Blantyre derby. Wanderers have won the last four derby games and did not lose any goal. Bullets want to stop this run. The match will be under lights at night.
en.body_more:
Bullets have lost four derby games in a row. Their coach Gilbert Chirwa said the team has worked hard to play better. In their first two games this season, Bullets drew 2-2 with Civil Service United and won 1-0 against Mafco. Chirwa said his players made many chances but did not score enough goals. He said they are working on this.

Wanderers played only one league game so far. They beat Creck Sporting Club 2-1. Their assistant coach McDonald Mtetemera said the team is ready and the fans will see good football. Wanderers' head coach Bob Mpinganjira just came back from the United Kingdom, so Mtetemera will run the team on Sunday.

One key player is Sama Thierry Tanjong from Wanderers. He is from Cameroon and has scored in all four recent derby wins. Bullets' captain Blessing Mpokera will try to stop him. Bullets also have new players. Clever Kalambo and George Chaomba scored in their first games. The match will be the official start of the FDH Bank Premiership season. The league said no glass bottles or drinks can be sold at the stadium.

ny.title: Bullets ndi Wanderers adzu pa Chikondano Lachilungamo
ny.body: Mighty Wanderers ndi FCB Nyasa Bullets adzalumikizana pa Lacha Lachilungamo pa Lisungu pakhomo la Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe. Wanderers awonedwa ndi anzawo zisanu zina ndizina. Bullets akufuna kuzimitsa izi. Masewera adzakhalira ndi magetsi usiku.
ny.body_more:
Bullets awona kusowa masewero anayi motsatatsatana. Mlengelezi wa Bullets Gilbert Chirwa ananena kuti anthu ake anagwira ntchito yayinzake. Pa masewero awiri oyamba, Bullets anagwirizana ndi Civil Service United 2-2 ndipo ananyamula Mafco 1-0. Chirwa ananena kuti anthu ake adapanga mwayi zambiri koma sangayerekezepo mwachinsinsi. Iye anati anthu ake akugwira ntchito pa ine iyi.

Wanderers anagwira masewero imodzi yokha mu nyengo. Iwo ananyamula Creck Sporting Club 2-1. Mlengelezi wachiwiri wa Wanderers McDonald Mtetemera anati gulu lake likugwira ntchito bwino ndipo anthu adzawona masewerewa abwino. Mlengelezi wakulu wa Wanderers Bob Mpinganjira kunse kwa dziko, chifukwa chake Mtetemera azatha magwiro pa Lisungu.

Mwalaleza wina wotengeka ndi Sama Thierry Tanjong kwa Wanderers. Iye akuchokera ku Cameroon ndipo asungulako mu masewero anayi abwino. Mlengelezi wa Bullets Blessing Mpokera azayesa kumusunganiza. Bullets alipo ndi anthu atsopano. Clever Kalambo ndi George Chaomba adasungulako poseweramo koyamba. Masewera adzalamulira koyambirira nyengo ya FDH Bank Premiership. Gulumende la masewera linati vilase ndi zakumwa sizisenda pa makhomo a msewero.

en.title: Malawi loses to Nigeria, misses World Cup
en.body: Malawi's Under-20 women's football team lost to Nigeria 2-1 yesterday at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. The loss ends Malawi's chance to go to the FIFA Under-20 Women's World Cup in Poland. Malawi had lost the first game 2-0 in Lagos one week before. Forward Faith Chinzimu scored two goals for Malawi, but Nigeria scored one goal in the second half to win the tie.
en.body_more:
Chinzimu, who could not play in the first game because of club work, scored just ten minutes into the match. She got the ball from a free-kick by Olivia Phikani and shot past Nigeria's goalkeeper Christiana Ijoma Uzoma. The home crowd was very happy. Malawi was ahead 1-0 at half-time after the goalie Emily Nkhwazi made two big saves.

Chinzimu scored again in the 55th minute. She took the ball from a Nigeria player and ran from halfway up the field into the box. She shot and scored. Malawi now led 2-0. But four minutes later, Nigeria's Precious Chimere Oscar scored to make it 2-1. This goal ended Malawi's hope.

Malawi coach Maggie Chombo-Sadik said her team made a bad mistake that let Nigeria score. She had two foreign players back for this game—Chinzimu, who plays in Sweden, and Rose Kabzere. Before this match, Malawi had won all four rounds to reach this final round. They beat Central African Republic, Sudan, South Sudan, and Guinea Bissau on aggregate. Nigeria's coach Moses Adukwu said both teams played well.

The World Cup will be held in Poland in September this year. Only Malawi and Nigeria were left in this part of the qualifiers for Africa. Nigeria only needed a draw to qualify, but Malawi needed to win by three goals. The Young Scorchers will now not play in the World Cup for the first time.

ny.title: Malawi anapoteza ndi Nigeria, sana World Cup
ny.body: Timu ya Malawi ya zisukulu za m'bado zapakati (Under-20) pa masewera a mpira yapoteza ndi Nigeria 2-1 djolo lero pa Bingu National Stadium mu Lilongwe. Mupulakilo uwu umapela mwayi wa Malawi kupita ku FIFA Under-20 Women's World Cup mu Poland. Malawi inali atapoteza papepala poyamba ndi 2-0 mu Lagos sabata imadzi. Njira yoweruka Faith Chinzimu adasunga magoli awiri pa Malawi, koma Nigeria inasunga goal imodzi mu papala lachiwiri ndipo itankhaka mphela.
ny.body_more:
Chinzimu, amene sanayesere papala poyamba chifukwa cha ntchito ya club, anasungila goal yapoyamba miniti khumi pokaposa. Anapeza mpira kuchokera ku free-kick wa Olivia Phikani ndipo anatumiza kwa mlibisi wa Nigeria Christiana Ijoma Uzoma. Anthu amomwe anali amwali kwambiri. Malawi inali pambu 1-0 nthawi yotengana mphala m'mbuyo mu pamphala loyamba, mulimosela Emily Nkhwazi anapangitsa misampha iwiri yaikulu.

Chinzimu anasungila goal lachiwiri mu miniti ya 55. Anatenga mpira ku oyendera wa Nigeria ndipo anthamanga kuchokera pakati pa bwalo kukhala mu bokisi la goal. Anatumiza mpira ndipo anasungila. Malawi inakwera 2-0. Koma miniti ina yapitapo, njira ya Nigeria yemwe iye Precious Chimere Oscar anasungila goal kuti ikhale 2-1. Goalelo inayimitsitsa mwayi wa Malawi.

Kowezezo wa Malawi Maggie Chombo-Sadik anati timu yake idalakwira mwanzo pomwe analola Nigeria kusungila goal. Anali ndi majambula awiri ochoka kunja pa mapepala awa—Chinzimu amene amayendera mu Sweden ndi Rose Kabzere. Mamwayi ameneyu, Malawi inapambana mapepala osakanika amatayi pofika apalemba. Inabwinja Central African Republic, Sudan, South Sudan, ndi Guinea Bissau mu pimphala lonthu. Kowezezo wa Nigeria Moses Adukwu anati timu zonse zinadlira bwino.

World Cup idzachitika mu Poland mu September chaka ichi. Malawi ndi Nigeria zonse zonse zidali zatsala. Nigeria inakufuna kokha kuti ipite, koma Malawi inakufuna kuwina ndi magoli atatu osapembedwa. Young Scorchers sizipitabe ndi nyengo ziyi ku World Cup.
